samples.gvd:
module asyn_fifo #(
    parameter DEPTH = 16,
    parameter WIDTH = 8
)(
    input wclk,
    input wenc,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] wptr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] din,
    input rclk,
    input renc,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] rptr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] dout
);

reg [WIDTH-1:0] ram [DEPTH-1:0];

always @(posedge wclk) begin
    if (wenc) begin
        ram[wptr] <= din;
    end
end

always @(posedge rclk) begin
    if (renc) begin
        dout <= ram[rptr];
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Asynchronous FIFO is composed of four parts. First is dual-port RAM. The FIFO data is stored in the RAM. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule. The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata.

Second part is data write controller. It contains write pointer, write full, write address and write increment. The write pointer is incremented by write increment. If FIFO is not full, write data to RAM.

Third part is data read controller. It contains read pointer, read empty, read address and read increment. The read pointer is incremented by read increment. If FIFO is not empty, read data from RAM.

Fourth part is read pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of write clock. The read pointer is collected by two-stage trigger of write clock. The read pointer is output to data write controller.

Fifth part is write pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of read clock. The write pointer is collected by two-stage trigger of read clock. The write pointer is output to data read controller.

Full and empty signals are generated by comparing the Gray code of read pointer and write pointer. The Gray code is converted to binary number. The lower bits of binary number are the read address or write address.
